---
title: "Lecture 15: Control Flow with Conditionals"
date: 2026-07-08
summary: "Writing conditionals with if, else, and else if"
url_slides: 15-slides.qmd
url_code: css-materials/if-else
page-layout: full
draft: false
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE)
```

## Info and Materials

* **Date**: {{< meta date >}}
* **Topics**: {{< meta summary >}}
* **Slides**: To be posted before class
* **Code**: To be posted before class

<!--
* **Slides**: [View Slides]({{< meta url_slides >}})
* **Code**: `usethis::use_course("{{< meta url_code >}}")`
-->


## Learning Objectives

- Define what control structures are and why they matter
- Introduce one type of control structure: conditional statements


## Readings

- [Chapter 13 “Control Structures”](https://bookdown.org/rdpeng/rprogdatascience/control-structures.html) in "R programming for Data Science" (Notice this is not our regular book!)
